subject: ap-chemistry
title: Thermochemistry & Calorimetry
stimulus:
4Fe(s) + 3O (g) → 2Fe O (s) ∆H° = − 1650kJ/mol
2 2 3 rxn
4. A student investigates a reaction used in hand warmers, represented above. The student mixes Fe(s ) with a
catalyst and sand in a small open container. The student measures the temperature of the mixture as the
reaction proceeds. The data are given in the following table.
Time (min) Temperature of Mixture (°C)
0 22.0
1 25.1
2 34.6
3 37.3
4 39.7
5 39.4

parts.prompt:
The mixture (Fe(s ), catalyst, and sand) has a total mass of 15.0 g and a specific heat capacity of
0.72 J/( g°C). Calculate the amount of heat absorbed by the mixture from 0 minutes to 4 minutes.

scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated value with units: 1 point

required_elements: For the correct calculated value with units: 1 point

parts.prompt:
Calculate the mass of Fe(s ), in grams, that reacted to generate the amount of heat calculated in part (a).
Use a pencil or pen with black or dark blue ink only. Do NOT write your name. Do NOT write outside the box.

scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated value of the moles of reaction, consistent with part (a) (may be 1 point

required_elements: For the correct calculated value of the moles of reaction, consistent with part (a) (may be 1 point

scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated value of the mass of iron: 1 point

required_elements: For the correct calculated value of the mass of iron: 1 point
alternatives.correct_example: 4 mol Fe 55.85 g Fe0.00012 mol 0.027 g Fe1 mol 1 mol Ferxn rxn×× = (0.026 g if decimals are carried)

parts.prompt:
In a second experiment, the student uses twice the mass of iron as that calculated in part (b) but the same
mass of sand as in the first experiment. Would the maximum temperature reached in the second experiment be
greater than, less than, or equal to the maximum temperature in the first experiment? Justify your answer.
Use a pencil or pen with black or dark blue ink only. Do NOT write your name. Do NOT write outside the box.

scoring_points.description: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point

required_elements: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point
